channel.title: Fallout on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Fallout on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Fallout vs. Arcanum Story States
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout versus Arcanum’s story states.

item.title: Fallout: Destiny Or Preparation?
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about whether Fallout was destined to happen or the result of massive preparation.

item.title: A Typical Day Of Making Fallout
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about a typical day of making Fallout, kind of to give you an insight into what it was like.

item.title: Non-Expository Fallout Lore
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, for a fun Friday, I wanted to talk about nonexpository Fallout lore.

item.title: Fallout Lore Exploration
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout lore exploration.

item.title: Fallout's Memory Model
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout’s memory model.

item.title: Fallout Foot Sliding: A Case Study in R&D
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout Footsliding, which I think is a good case study of R&D, especially in game development in the 1980s and 1990s.

item.title: Challenges Making A Fallout Remaster
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the challenges making of making a Fallout remaster.

item.title: Alternate Fallout Radiation Design
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about an alternate Fallout radiation design.

item.title: Real-Time Fallout
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about realtime Fallout.

item.title: Why I Wanted Luck In Fallout
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about why I wanted luck in Fallout.

item.title: Photos From Fallout Development (1994-1997)
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about a collection of Fallout photos I have from 1994 through 1997.

item.title: Fallout's Starting Date
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about fallout’s starting date and specifically what I mean by that is: why is the original Fallout set 84 years after the war?

item.title: A Real Life Fallout Story With Lasers
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to tell you a real life Fallout story with lasers.

item.title: Pros And Cons Of GURPS As A CRPG
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the pros and cons of gerps as a computer role playing game, specifically what I learned trying to implement gerps as a computer game for Fallout.

item.title: Two Recent Mementos
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about two recent momentos I now have — and they’re behind me, and I’ll Point them out in a few minutes.

item.title: Fallout's Effect On Me
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about fallout’s effect on me, which seems like a very personal, self-reflected, reflective, almost narcissistic topic.

item.title: The Number Of Vaults In Fallout
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the number of vaults in Fallout.

item.title: Would I Work On Fallout Again?
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer the question: would I work on Fallout again?

item.title: The Canceling of Fallout Van Buren...And Me
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the cancellation of Fallout Van Buren and me.

item.title: Fallout As A Work Of Art
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout as a work of art, and just before I get started, I’m talking about the whole series.

item.title: Fallout And Wasteland
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout and Wasteland.

item.title: Fallout Easter Eggs In Other Games
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout, Easter eggs in other games.

item.title: Fallout TV Show Review
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me Tim. Let’s do a Fallout TV show review.

item.title: Fallout TV Show
item.description: Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim. Okay, let’s talk about the Fallout TV Show, because apparently my game development stories are not interesting to you people, so let me talk about it.

item.title: Fallout Development Timeline
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to do the Fallout development timeline.

item.title: Fallout 1 and 2 Box Covers
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the Fallout 1 and two box covers and yes, I’m calling fallout Fallout one, just so I don’t say I’m talking about the Fallout box covers and then everyone will get confused.

item.title: The Four Steps For Fixing Design Problems
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about fixing design problems.

item.title: The Origin of VATS
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the origin of Vats in Fallout and I’ll start by saying we didn’t call it bats in Fallout 1 and two.

item.title: Multiple Quest Solutions
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about multiple Quest Solutions and I want to use an example from Fallout that I used in my talk at the reboot conference in 2017.

item.title: The Story Of The Bag
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to tell a story about a picture that floats around on the internet of me with a paper bag on my head sitting at my desk.

item.title: Mementos: My T. Ray Isaac Original
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about another momento story.

item.title: The Fallout Team
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about the Fallout team.

item.title: Fallout 4 Launch Party
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the Fallout 4 launch party.

item.title: Fallout: The Original Intro Story
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about the original story intro to Fallout.

item.title: Fallout 3: Press Event and Launch Party
item.description: Hey everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout 3 and the E3 showing and the ship party

item.title: Mementos: The Fallout Foamcore Board
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about one of the items behind me.

item.title: Thoughts On A Fallout Remake
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer a question from Evan shantlin Who, full disclosure, worked on QA on Fallout.

item.title: Fallout: Nuka Break
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about Fallout — Nuka break.

item.title: Fallout Regrets
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer a question from commenter Brian Eno 608 better be a real name, don’t taunt me like that.

item.title: Games As Art (and thoughts on the Fallout TV Show)
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about games as art and the meaning of games, but it’s going to be a little rambling because I’m going to tell you how

item.title: The Six Demos Of Fallout
item.description: Hello everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about these six demos of Fallout.

item.title: Fallout: What I'd Change Today
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about what changes I would make to Fallout if I were to Tinker with it today.

item.title: Fallout Random Encounters
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout, Random Encounters and exactly how those things work.

item.title: Fallout Criticals
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to explain exactly how criticals work in Fallout, and by criticals I mean both critical successes and critical failures.

item.title: A Chat With Scott Campbell
item.description: Tim, today I’m talking with Scott Campbell, the original lead designer, Fallout. We’re going to talk about Fallout things and what he’s been up to for the last forever. Yeah, seven years. Yeah, long long time, so you were the, you were one of the earliest team members on Fallout. You started on the team, I would think right after the two jasons. You know, uh, that was when I officially started. But uh, yeah, I mean since, like you know, even when we were just, like you know, playing gurps just for fun, after hours, you know I was, I was there, um, but even then, um, you know, I I have an actual trivia question for you, Tim. Do you remember the first time we met? Do you remember this?

item.title: Fallout SPECIAL Attribute Effects
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the attributes in Fallout, the special attributes.

item.title: The Origin of Fallout's Setting
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the beginning, the creation of the origin of the Fallout setting.

item.title: Fallout: Radiation vs FEV
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about radiation and the fev virus in Fallout and before I start I just want to say

item.title: Fallout's Voice Overs
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about fallouts voice overs.

item.title: A Chat With Jason Taylor
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I’m going to chat with Jason Taylor, JT, who was the first person on what would later be called team Fallout.

item.title: The Credits Problem (e.g. Fallout Companions)
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about credits and how people get credited in games, and I’m going to do that by telling you a story, because I’m frequently asked: hey uh, who did the companions in Fallout?

item.title: The First Time Fallout Was Almost Canceled
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk to you about the first time Fallout almost got canceled.

item.title: The Biggest Influences On Fallout
item.description: Hi. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about what influenced the creation of Fallout.

item.title: The Sugar Bombs conspiracy...
item.description: Okay, people put on your foil conspiracy hats, as the skull Panda pointed out in the comments. Comments sugar bombs don’t appear as items in Fallout 1 and 2. But they do appear in my notes from the 90s and in reference to Calvin and Hobbes, the complete collection of which I own in the bookcase behind me. But they do appear in Fallout 3, New Vegas and for and Beyond. I have no explanation for this parallel design development. Mutual Calvin and Hobb fans.

item.title: GDC Showcase 2023
item.description: Hey everyone. I just wanted to announce that on Wednesday, June 28th at 1 pm, the GDC showcase is going to re-air my classic game postmortem on Fallout, and I will be doing a live chat at the same time, answering all the attendees Fallout related questions. There’s a link below, if you want to attend, thanks.

item.title: A Chat With Leonard Boyarsky, Part 2
item.description: Him and I’m joined again today for part two of my chat with Leonard bararski. Hey Leonard, hey Tim, how’s it going? I’m good.

item.title: Fallout Cut Content
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Fallout cut content.

item.title: Frequently Asked Questions
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim.

item.title: A Chat With Leonard Boyarsky
item.description: I have my first guest, a man who needs no introduction: no, it’s Leonard burarski. I’ve known him for 30 years. We’ve been working together for much of that time — not all of it. He had his own Adventures and he looks exactly the same, and I look like a picture of Dorian Gray, so that is. That is verifiably untrue. People can go Google and make their own decision.

item.title: Why I Left Fallout 2
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today let’s talk about Fallout 2 and why I left it I really think, if you haven’t, you should watch yesterday’s video.

item.title: Fallout Was A B-Tier Project
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim.

item.title: History of Fallout: The GURPS Apps
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I wanted to do a little bit of video game archeology or paleontology, I don’t know — demon ontology, because

item.title: How Fallout Got Its Name
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about how Fallout got its name.

item.title: The Music of Fallout
item.description: Hi. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about something that’s near and dear to me, which is fallout’s music.

item.title: How Fallout was made SPECIAL
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about how, in just two weeks, right near the end, about four or five months before Fallout shipped, we replaced gurps with special.

item.title: The True Purpose of Vaults in Fallout
item.description: Hi everyone, it’s me Tim. Wow, you are filling up my comments with so many good ideas for topics that I can’t even really keep up. I had planned today, uh, to either talk about Bloodlines, vampire Bloodlines and the cut content and some of our plans for the sequels, and then I thought, no, no, I’m gonna talk about music, and not just the great music in Fallout, but arcanum’s music and vampires music and the fact that I’m sad each and every day that I got to work with the composer, Johan Johansson.

item.title: The Origin of Dumb Dialog
item.description: Hi. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to tell a story.

item.title: Let's Talk Fallout 1
item.description: Hot, hi, it’s me Tim. Uh, before I start, um, that power armor helmet was made by Josh J, an artist on Fallout 4, and he sent it to me. How cool is that? Uh, yeah, it barely fits over my big head though. Um, it’s really fun though. It’s cool to have something like that.
